slides.headline: In Irish, caillte means “lost”.
slides.body: We named the company after the one thing we kill. Swipe.

slides.headline: Every missed call is a lost job.
slides.body: Not a maybe. The caller rings the next trade and books them instead.

slides.headline: You don’t lose the job on price. You lose it because you didn’t pick up.
You’re on the tools. The phone’s in the van.
slides.body: That’s not a you problem — it’s every trade’s problem. You can’t answer and earn at once.

slides.headline: So we built the receptionist that never sleeps.
slides.body: Answers every call, books the job, takes the deposit — while you stay on the job.

slides.headline: What are missed calls costing you?
slides.save: Save this — the next ring-out is a job
